_about: WITNESSED fak-authored measurement for the industry-scorecard rows kv-cache-aware-routing and kv-aware-routing-locality. The cross-instance (fleet) analogue of fak's on-instance FCFS 62.1% -> cache-aware 86.7% radix-hit recovery: it measures whether a KV-aware fleet router sends a request to the instance that already holds its prefix KV, vs a cache-blind round-robin balancer (what gateway.ReplicaRouter.pick does today).
engine: fak gateway KV-aware fleet router (internal/gateway/kv_fleet_routing.go): residency-aware placement + least-loaded cold homing, the cross-GPU composition of the on-instance residency signal
metric: cross-instance prefix-KV routing hit rate (fraction of requests routed to an instance already holding the prefix KV -> no cold re-prefill)

workload.name: shared-prefix agent fleet
workload.desc: Zipf-skewed cross-agent traffic over distinct shared-prefix families (system prompts / agent scaffolds), interleaved one-per-family-per-round (concurrent agents, not back-to-back bursts). Aggregate working set EXCEEDS one instance's resident capacity but FITS the fleet, so locality is what keeps the hot prefixes home.

competitor.name: Baseten on NVIDIA Dynamo (KV-aware router)
competitor.downstream_wallclock_effect: -50% TTFT / -34% TPOT / +62% output tok/s / +61% RPS on Qwen3 480B ~50k-token inputs (host-gated; NOT measured here)

competitor_band.metric: published cross-replica / cross-request prefix-KV cache-hit-rate band for cache-aware routers
competitor_band.points: Baseten-on-Dynamo 0.89 across 4 replicas; SGLang RadixAttention published 50-99% hit-rate band (arXiv:2312.07104)

fence: WITNESSED routing-DECISION hit rate from a deterministic simulation of the placement policy (no GPUs). It is the upstream CAUSE the SOTA wall-clock wins flow from; the downstream wall-clock TTFT/throughput win on real GPUs (Baseten -50% TTFT / +62% tok/s; GORGO 2.5x median TTFT) is host-gated and explicitly NOT claimed by this number. Same metric class as Baseten's live cross-replica cache hit, different apparatus (simulation vs live GPU fleet) -> apples_to_apples=false.
